item.title: Japan Stocks Set to Climb as Yen Hits 40-Year Low: Markets Wrap
item.description: Japanese stocks were set to rise as the yen slid to a four-decade low against the dollar and a rebound in several US technology giants lifted Wall Street.

item.title: Forgent, Backer Offer Stock After Price Doubles Since IPO
item.description: Forgent Power Solutions Inc. and its private equity backer Neos Partners LP are offering to sell about 35 million shares, marking the third stock sale since the power distribution equipment maker went public in February.

item.title: Oil Holds Gain as Iran Seeks Hormuz Control Before Talks With US
item.description: Oil held an advance, as a top Iranian official reiterated the country’s determination to maintain control over the Strait of Hormuz before fresh peace talks with the US.

item.title: Exiled Chinese Tycoon Guo Gets 30 Years in US Prison for Fraud
item.description: Exiled Chinese tycoon Miles Guo, who promoted himself as a crusader against the communist government in Beijing, was ordered to spend 30 years in a US prison for duping investors out of $1 billion.

item.title: Grasshopper Club Zurich Sold by LA Team to Bridge Football
item.description: Bridge Football Group has bought Grasshopper Club Zurich, one of Switzerland’s most storied football teams.

item.title: Supreme Court Affirms Presidential Power, with a Carve-Out for the Fed
item.description: The Supreme Court blocked President Trump’s attempt to remove Fed Governor Lisa Cook for now while expanding his power to fire other officials. What this means for the Fed and others, on the Big Take podcast.

item.title: Takaichi Aims to Shape Legacy With Unprecedented Economic Plan
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is staking her legacy as Japan’s first female premier on an unprecedented plan to reshape the country’s industrial base for decades to come.

item.title: South Korean Financial Firms Fight for Share of AI Pay Bonanza
item.description: South Koreans are getting richer more quickly than anyone else in the world, creating an unprecedented opportunity for the country’s asset managers.

item.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.description: 円が40年ぶり安値、米イラン協議、FRBの独立性、ウォール街沸く、ビットコインETFから資金流出

item.title: Susquehanna Says It Lost Millions to Mystery Insider Traders
item.description: Susquehanna Investment Group is attempting to unmask the identities of individuals it claims made at least $100 million trading on inside information about a Chinese government crackdown on cross-border brokerages last month.

item.title: BofA to Pay SEC $7.5 Million Over Reporting Lapse Allegations
item.description: Bank of America Corp. agreed to pay $7.5 million to the Securities and Exchange Commission to settle allegations that its Merrill Lynch brokerage failed to properly file all required suspicious activity reports, according to a administrative action filed Monday.

item.title: JPMorgan Says Private Muni-Bond Accounts Swell to $1.6 Trillion
item.description: The business of overseeing individually tailored municipal-bond portfolios has continued to grow rapidly, turning those money managers into the biggest holders of state and local government debt, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co.

item.title: Six Takeaways From the Supreme Court’s Fed Independence Ruling
item.description: The US Supreme Court on Monday gave President Donald Trump robust legal backing to fire top agency officials, with one glaring exception: the Federal Reserve.

item.title: Argentine Economy Contracted in April as Uneven Growth Persists
item.description: Argentina’s economy contracted in April after a short-lived rebound the previous month, underscoring a trend of uneven growth more than halfway through President Javier Milei’s term.

item.title: NZ Rate Hike Bets Dialed Back on US-Iran Peace Progress
item.description: New Zealand economists are dialing back expectations for a Reserve Bank interest-rate increase next week, saying early signs of peace between the US and Iran have eased inflation risks enough to justify keeping borrowing costs on hold.

item.title: Lagarde Says Europe Is Getting More Resilient to Economic Shocks
item.description: Europe is becoming less vulnerable to outside shocks thanks to a better financial framework and progress on the green transition, Christine Lagarde said.

item.title: Uber and Waymo Have Ended Their Robotaxi Tie-Up in Phoenix; Uber to Announce New Partner
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. said it has wound down its robotaxi offering with Alphabet Inc.’s Waymo in Phoenix, the latest relationship twist between two companies that simultaneously function as partners and competitors.

item.title: Alibaba, Tencent Dropped by DC Lobbyists to Comply With US Curbs
item.description: Washington’s most powerful lobbying firms are rushing to cut ties with Chinese tech giants including Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. and Tencent Holdings Ltd. as new US restrictions reshape the influence game for many Chinese companies.

item.title: BofA Technician Sees a ‘Three-Wave Correction’ in S&P 500 Index
item.description: Investors must hedge any further S&P 500 rallies and brace for a potential “three-wave correction” in the next few months, according to the head of technical research at Bank of America Corp.

item.title: Super Micro Office Raided as Taiwan Expands Chip Smuggling Probe
item.description: Super Micro Computer Inc.’s offices in Taiwan were raided by government authorities on Monday, widening an investigation into the alleged smuggling of Nvidia Corp. chips into China using the company’s servers, according to a person familiar with the situation.

item.title: JPMorgan Struggles to Find Demand for Oil Driller’s 15% Loan
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has struggled to attract investor demand for a $775 million loan to a Trump administration-supported oil driller that would pay a hefty 15% interest rate, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Charter Junk Bonds, CDS See Record Moves on Bets of Comcast Deal
item.description: Some Charter Communications Inc. debt securities saw record improvement Monday, as traders bet that rival Comcast Corp.’s pending breakup could result in a combination of the two broadband heavyweights.

item.title: Soaring US Equity Funding Costs Risk Spilling Over to Repo Rates
item.description: US equity financing needs have been building up and risk crowding out capacity in bank dealers’ balance sheets, which in turn could boost short-term interest rates even amid limited quarter-end pressures.

item.title: Mexico Central Bank Adds Bond-Buying Tool to Support Liquidity
item.description: Mexico’s central bank published regulations allowing it to buy some local government securities to boost money-market liquidity if needed, a possible preemptive move to help the regulator step in if investors are forced to sell Mexican assets.

item.title: Supreme Court Says Warrant Needed for Phone Location Data
item.description: The Supreme Court placed limits on a law enforcement investigative technique that leverages data on cell phone users’ location history to identify people near a crime scene.

item.title: France, Oman Back Free Transit Through Hormuz Without Conditions
item.description: France’s Emmanuel Macron and Oman’s Sultan Haitham bin Tariq said that they would promote restriction-free transit in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: HDFC Bank Appoints Ex-Bureaucrat Rajiv Kumar as Part-Time Chair
item.description: HDFC Bank Ltd. named former finance secretary, Rajiv Kumar, as its part-time chairman, months after the abrupt resignation of Atanu Chakraborty sparked questions about governance at the nation’s largest private lender.

item.title: MrBeast, Top YouTubers Sell Their Scraps at New Age Auction
item.description: Bidders accustomed to buying prized art or luxury goods were recently introduced to a new category of coveted items at auction: Mementos from the world’s most popular internet stars.

item.title: Fossil Fuel Subsidies to Hit $1 Trillion in War’s Energy Price Shock, UN Says
item.description: Fossil fuel subsidies are projected to reach $1.1 trillion in 2026 as governments spend more to shield consumers from high energy prices triggered by the war in Iran.

item.title: Siris-Backed TPx Files Bankruptcy, Mulls $1 Billion Debt Cut
item.description: Technology solutions provider U.S. TelePacific, also known as TPx Communications, filed for bankruptcy in Texas on Sunday after undergoing repeated debt restructuring while struggling to stay liquid.
